name: Chicken and Sweet Pepper Stir-Fry
recipeInstructions:
1. Thinly slice the skinless chicken into 1/2-inch pieces and place in a bowl. Stir in soy sauce and sherry. Let stand for 30 minutes.
2. Spray a cold wok or large skillet with Pam; preheat over medium-high heat.
3. Add onion to the wok and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
4. Add peppers to the wok and stir-fry for 1 minute.
5. Add mushrooms to the wok and stir-fry for about 1 more minute, or until vegetables are crisp-tender. Remove veggies from the wok and set aside.
6. Drain the chicken, reserving the marinade. Add oil to the wok. Add ginger root and stir-fry for 15 seconds.
7. Add half of the chicken to the wok and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until no longer pink. Remove from the wok.
8. Stir-fry the remaining chicken for 3-4 minutes until no longer pink. Return all the chicken, veggies, and bamboo shoots to the wok.
9. Push everything from the center of the wok. Stir broth, cornstarch, and 1/4 teaspoon pepper into the reserved marinade; add to the wok.
10. Cook and stir until slightly thickened, tossing gently to coat the chicken mixture. Serve while hot.
